How Bus travel time is affected by multiple factors

Introduction:

This visual data story explores factors that influence bus travel times based on a data set of logged bus journeys. The data set includes information such as travel time, number of stops,delay reasons and the time and day of each trip occurred.

The aim of this is investigate how bus travel time changes depending on different conditions,including delay cause,time of day, and day of the week. By transforming the time stamp data into a usable variable by using the lubricate package, it was possible to examine patterns across both hourly and weekly cycles.

These visualizations are used to explore these relationships and identify when and why bus travel times tend to increase and when is the best time to catch the bus.

Graph one:

Plot that shows how diffrent conditions and which affects average bus travel time the most

Text for explanation: Plot that shows how different conditions like how delays affects average bus travel time,heavy traffic and amount of passengers boarding or exiting with heavy traffic being the cause for the longest average bus travel time, which tends to be above 60 minutes. No conditions tends to have the quickest average bus travel time which tends to be on average under 60 mintues. Passangers are the second condtion that affects the average bus travel time by being above 60 mintues and yet below heavy traffic.

Graph 2:

Boxplot showing bus travel time across different weekdays, highlighting variation and spread in travel times with some days showing higher delays than others

Text for explanation: Plot that shows how Bus travel times changes across the week. Thursday has the lowest average bus travel time which suggest that taking the bus on Thursday is the best for the quickest travel time. There is high variation and spread across the days of the week. Both Wednesday and Tuesday are likely to have higher travel times on average compared to both Monday and Thursday, however it also tends to be slightly higher compared to Friday. This suggest that Friday will tend to have longer average travel times during the week compared to every other day.

Graph 3:

Scatter plot showing bus travel time against hour of day, with points coloured by delay reason to show how delays vary across different times

Text for explanation: Plot that shows how bus travel time varies depending on the time of day. Each point represents an individual trip while the color indicates the type of delays experienced. We can observe whether certain times of day tend to have longer travel times, which may suggest peak-hour congestion or other time-related effects on bus performance. The different delay categories also help show whether specific types of disruption are more common at certain hours. As we can see, None tends to on average have the lowest travel time based on hour of the day with the highest being heavy traffic followed up by passangers boarding and exiting
